_note: Protocols shaping the agentic era. 2026-Q2 snapshot.

items.name: MCP — Model Context Protocol
items.primaryJob: Connect agents to tools, data sources, and apps

adoption.note: Cross-vendor adoption — Anthropic, Microsoft, OpenAI (via Apps SDK), Google (via Vertex). 350+ servers tracked.

lagsAt: Auth / multi-tenancy still evolving (RFC 2.0 in flight)
axes.verdict: Default for tool connection. The de-facto standard.

items.name: A2A — Agent-to-Agent Protocol
items.primaryJob: Discover and delegate to other agents

adoption.note: Useful for multi-agent systems and sub-task delegation. Different problem space than MCP.

axes.verdict: Use when you're orchestrating multiple agents. Complementary to MCP, not a replacement.

items.name: Apps SDK
items.primaryJob: OpenAI's framework for building apps inside ChatGPT

adoption.note: Uses MCP-style server protocol for tools; ChatGPT-specific UI / widget rendering layered on top.

openSpec.note: Tool layer is MCP; UI / widget hooks are ChatGPT-specific.

leadsAt: Builds on MCP rather than replacing it
axes.verdict: Default if your audience lives in ChatGPT and you want first-class UI in the chat surface.

items.name: Agent Protocol
items.primaryJob: Vendor-neutral REST API for invoking agents

adoption.note: Less momentum than MCP / A2A. Worth tracking as a hedge against vendor protocols fragmenting.

axes.verdict: Worth tracking. Default to MCP for now; revisit Agent Protocol if vendor protocols fragment.
